text:
The [applicant] / [respondent]’s solicitor is permitted to disclose a copy of this order to [police force] Police for the purposes of the request for disclosure under the protocol.

Disclosure of this order

Permission is given to the parties to disclose this order, redacted to remove any parts of the order not relevant for the purpose of such disclosure, to any third party from whom information/reports/evidence is directed.

Participation directions / Ground Rules / Special Measures

The [applicant] / [respondent] has made [an] allegation[s] of domestic abuse. The [applicant] / [respondent] is therefore vulnerable for the purposes of FPR Part 3A and their participation in the proceedings is likely to be affected.

The court considers that it may be necessary to make participation directions in respect of a party or witness.

The court has concluded that [name] is [vulnerable] / [a protected party] and that [his] / [her] participation in the proceedings is likely to be affected because:

it is alleged that [name] is the victim of domestic abuse;

the court considers that the quality of evidence given by [name] is likely to be assisted by the services of an intermediary who can facilitate and enable communication and understanding of questions to and answers by a witness or party;

[insert other reason].

If they have not already done so, any party who considers that specific measures need to be taken to enable a party or witness to understand the proceedings and their role in them when in court, put their views to the court, instruct their representatives before, during, and after the hearing or attend the hearing without significant distress should file an application notice and include the following information as far as practicable:

why the party or witness would benefit from assistance;

the measure or measures that would be likely to maximise as far as practicable the quality of their evidence or participation and why;

written confirmation from any relevant witness of his/her views.

The court has concluded that there are currently no parties or witnesses for whom it is necessary to make participation directions because:

no matters have been drawn to the attention of the court and it is aware of no reasons which would lead it to conclude otherwise;

it has considered the matters adduced by [name] and concluded that the facilities available at court should afford sufficient protection.

metadata.source: Standard Family Orders — Volume 2: Children and Other Orders (May 2026)
